data.title: Microservices Infrastructure
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to manage different functionalities like messaging, notifications, and user authentication separately, providing scalability and maintenance ease.
data.technical_spec: Architecture style: Microservices; Communication: REST/GRPC; Scalability: Horizontal; Deployment: Containers/Kubernetes

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Built-in end-to-end encryption ensures that messages are securely transmitted between users without interception.
data.technical_spec: Protocols: Signal Protocol for encryption; Key Management: Asymmetric cryptography; Storage: Encrypted storage on device

data.title: Global Distributed Servers
data.description: A network of distributed servers globally to ensure reliable and fast message delivery across different regions.
data.technical_spec: Distribution: Global CDN; Load Balancing: Geo-based routing; Redundancy: High availability clusters

data.title: Real-Time Messaging Protocol
data.description: Real-time messaging protocol enables instant sending and receiving of messages across platforms.
data.technical_spec: Protocol: WebSocket for real-time communication; Backup: HTTP fallback; Efficiency: Low-latency message dispatch

data.title: Cross-Platform Compatibility
data.description: Supports a wide range of devices and operating systems to ensure broad accessibility.
data.technical_spec: Compatibility: iOS, Android, Web; Frameworks: React Native for mobile; Integration: Seamless sync across platforms
